Room.title: Room
Room.description: Represents a physical consulting room within the clinic where appointments are held.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Room entity.

name.description: The display name of the consulting room, e.g., 'Consultorio 1'.

order.description: An integer defining the display order of the room in UI lists or grids.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the room record was created.

createdBy.description: Reference to the User ID who created this room record. (Relationship: User 1:N Room)

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the room record was last updated.

updatedBy.description: Reference to the User ID who last updated this room record. (Relationship: User 1:N Room)

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application, linked to an external authentication system (e.g., Firebase Auth), and stores their role and basic profile information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity, typically corresponding to the authentication system's user ID (e.g., Firebase Auth UID).

displayName.description: The full display name of the user.

role.description: The assigned role of the user (e.g., 'admin', 'doctor').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user record was last updated.

Doctor.title: Doctor
Doctor.description: Represents a medical doctor who performs consultations in the clinic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Doctor entity.

displayName.description: The full name or display name of the doctor.

specialty.description: The medical specialty of the doctor, if any.

userId.description: Reference to the associated User ID if the doctor has an application account. (Relationship: User 1:1 Doctor)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the doctor record was created.

createdBy.description: Reference to the User ID who created this doctor record. (Relationship: User 1:N Doctor)

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the doctor record was last updated.

updatedBy.description: Reference to the User ID who last updated this doctor record. (Relationship: User 1:N Doctor)

Booking.title: Booking
Booking.description: Represents a scheduled appointment or reservation for a consulting room by a doctor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Booking entity.

roomId.description: Reference to the Room ID where the booking takes place. (Relationship: Room 1:N Booking)

doctorId.description: Reference to the Doctor ID who made or is assigned to the booking. (Relationship: Doctor 1:N Booking)

dateKey.description: The date of the booking in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format, local to America/Mexico_City, used for efficient queries and overlap validation.

startAt.description: Full timestamp marking the start of the booking.

endAt.description: Full timestamp marking the end of the booking. Must be on the same dateKey as startAt.

startMin.description: The start time of the booking expressed as minutes from the beginning of the day (0-1439). Used for overlap calculations.

endMin.description: The end time of the booking expressed as minutes from the beginning of the day (0-1439). Used for overlap calculations.

status.description: The current status of the booking (e.g., 'reserved', 'confirmed', 'in_use', 'done', 'cancelled').

notes.description: Optional additional notes or comments related to the booking.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the booking record was created.

createdBy.description: Reference to the User ID who created this booking record. (Relationship: User 1:N Booking)

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the booking record was last updated.

updatedBy.description: Reference to the User ID who last updated this booking record. (Relationship: User 1:N Booking)

definition.description: User profile documents, including their assigned role and basic profile information. Access is restricted to the owner (matching `userId` with `request.auth.uid`) for read/write, and admins can read/write all. This collection serves as the source of truth for user roles.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: A collection used for Role-Based Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document with `adminId` matching `request.auth.uid` grants administrative privileges across the application. Only existing administrators can create or delete documents here. This uses the 'Existence over Content' pattern for global roles.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier of an administrator, corresponding to their Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Details about each physical consulting room in the clinic. All authenticated users can read room information (e.g., for display on dashboards or selection). Only administrators are authorized to create, update, or delete room records.

params.name: roomId
params.description: The unique identifier for a consulting room.

definition.description: Profiles of medical doctors. All authenticated users can read doctor profiles (e.g., for selection in booking forms). Only administrators are authorized to create, update, or delete doctor profiles. This entity includes `userId` to link to an application user account if the doctor is also a user.

params.name: doctorId
params.description: The unique identifier for a medical doctor.

definition.description: Scheduled appointments or reservations for consulting rooms by doctors. This is a top-level collection to facilitate efficient queries by `roomId` and `dateKey` for the dashboard, and by `doctorId` and `dateKey` for doctor-specific agendas. Read access is allowed for administrators, and for doctors where `request.auth.uid` matches `resource.data.doctorId`. Writes are primarily enforced via Cloud Functions for business logic (overlap validation, status changes); direct client-side writes are allowed for administrators. Includes denormalized `doctorId` and `createdBy` fields for authorization independence and queryability.

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique identifier for an appointment booking.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. It prioritizes Authorization Independence through strategic denormalization and leverages Structural Segregation to simplify security rules.   

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & Denormalization Strategy:**
1.  **For `Bookings`:** Each `Booking` document will include `doctorId` and `createdBy` fields. These fields directly store the UID of the doctor associated with the booking and the UID of the user who created it, respectively. This denormalization is crucial for Authorization Independence. Security rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.doctorId` or `resource.data.createdBy` without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent `Doctor` or `User` documents. This enables atomic operations and simplifies rule logic considerably, making them robust and easy to debug.
2.  **For Global Roles (`Admin`):** Instead of storing the role only within the `/users/{userId}` document and requiring a `get()` to check it, a dedicated top-level collection `/roles_admin/{adminId}` will be used. An admin's UID will exist as a document ID in this collection (e.g., `/roles_admin/someAdminUid`). Rule checks for admin privileges will then use `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`. While this is a `get()` operation, it's a single, direct, global check for a role marker, not a hierarchical dependency on a parent document's data, thus adhering to the spirit of Authorization Independence for global roles by using 'Existence over Content'.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) & Structural Segregation:**
1.  **`/users/{userId}`:** This collection stores individual user profiles and their assigned `role`. Access is strictly path-based: `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}` for a user to read/write their own profile. Admins are granted full access. This ensures that users can only query their own data effectively without filtering, and administrators can query all.
2.  **`/roles_admin/{adminId}`:** This collection serves as a direct, explicit mechanism for determining admin status. Rules will allow operations for users whose `request.auth.uid` exists in this collection. It's segregated to provide a homogeneous security posture for global admin checks.
3.  **`/rooms/{roomId}`:** `Room` entities are generally readable by all authenticated users (for display on dashboards, selection in forms). Write access (create, update, delete) is restricted to administrators. This segregation ensures a clear security posture for these administrative-only data modifications.
4.  **`/doctors/{doctorId}`:** `Doctor` entities are also generally readable by all authenticated users (e.g., for selection in booking forms). Write access is restricted to administrators. This collection maintains a uniform security posture, separating administrative data from user-specific data.
5.  **`/bookings/{bookingId}`:** This is a top-level collection crucial for queryability. Doctors need to view *their* bookings, and the dashboard needs to view bookings by `roomId` and `dateKey`. By including `doctorId` directly in the `Booking` document, rules can securely enforce that a doctor can only `list` documents where `resource.data.doctorId == request.auth.uid`. Similarly, the `roomId` and `dateKey` fields support efficient queries for the dashboard without relying on security rules to filter results, which is key for QAPs. Most critical writes to `/bookings` are mandated to go through Cloud Functions, which act as a trusted intermediary, enforcing business logic (overlap validation, status transitions) and fine-grained role checks. Direct client-side writes for non-admin users will be blocked by Firestore rules, enforcing the use of secure backend logic. Admins will retain direct write access for maximum flexibility if needed, but the primary flow will be via Cloud Functions for integrity.

This design leverages explicit data fields (`doctorId`, `createdBy`, `role` indirectly via `/roles_admin`) to make authorization decisions directly on the `resource.data` or `request.auth.uid`, minimizing reliance on complex `get()` calls in rules and maximizing clarity and debuggability. Structural segregation ensures that each collection has a consistent security profile, further simplifying rule logic.
